clusters.title: Israel conducts deadly attacks on southern Lebanon after Trump promises de-escalation

items.title: Israel conducts deadly attacks on southern Lebanon after Trump promises de-escalation
items.standfirst: Israel ​kept up strikes on southern Lebanon on Tuesday, killing eight people, including children, as it pressed its campaign against Hezbollah a day after President Donald Trump asked Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to attack Beirut to avert a further escalation.
items.content: Israel ​kept up strikes on southern Lebanon on Tuesday, killing eight people, including children, as it pressed its campaign against Hezbollah a day after President Donald Trump asked Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to attack Beirut to avert a further escalation.

items.title: Protests over US Ebola site in Kenya kill two, court keeps block
items.standfirst: A Kenyan court blocked on Tuesday for another three weeks a proposed US Ebola quarantine facility that has triggered protests killing two people. The proposed 50-bed unit on an air force base in central Kenya for Americans exposed to the ​virus in Democratic ‌Republic of Congo or Uganda has angered many Kenyans. They accuse the US of offloading the health ⁠risk of caring for patients.

clusters.title: Iowa shootings: gunman suspected of killing six relatives then himself

items.title: Iowa shootings: gunman suspected of killing six relatives then himself
items.standfirst: Police say four found dead in a home in Muscatine and two were shot elsewhere in apparent ‘domestic-related dispute’A mass shooting in Iowa on Monday, in which six people were killed by a man who then took his own life, is being investigated as a so-called “family annihilation” in which the murderer and victims are closely related.Police in Muscatine, about 50 miles (80km) south-east of Cedar Rapids, said 52-year-old Ryan Willis McFarland fatally shot four people at a home in the city, then killed himself on a riverfront trail as officers spoke to him. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: WHO reports dramatic drop in suspected Ebola cases from over 900 to 116

items.title: WHO reports dramatic drop in suspected Ebola cases from over 900 to 116
items.standfirst: The World Health Organization (WHO) on Tuesday reported a drop in suspected Ebola cases to 116, down from 906 last week. Testing had "cleared out" some on the suspected cases list. Around 321 cases were confirmed in the DR Congo, including 48 deaths, while nine confirmed cases have been registered in neighbouring Uganda, including one fatality.
items.content: The World Health Organization (WHO) on Tuesday reported a drop in suspected Ebola cases to 116, down from 906 last week. Testing had "cleared out" some on the suspected cases list. Around 321 cases were confirmed in the DR Congo, including 48 deaths, while nine confirmed cases have been registered in neighbouring Uganda, including one fatality.

items.title: Ebola recoveries bring signs of hope in DRC
items.standfirst: Four nurses who were being treated for Ebola caused by the Bundibugyo strain of the virus have been discharged from ​a hospital in ‌Bunia in the Democratic Republic of Congo after recovering from the disease, the World ⁠Health Organization said on Sunday. More recoveries are expected, especially when people are diagnosed early and able to access care, and as the response to the outbreak intensifies.
items.content: Four nurses who were being treated for Ebola caused by the Bundibugyo strain of the virus have been discharged from ​a hospital in ‌Bunia in the Democratic Republic of Congo after recovering from the disease, the World ⁠Health Organization said on Sunday. More recoveries are expected, especially when people are diagnosed early and able to access care, and as the response to the outbreak intensifies.

items.standfirst: Markets take note as world’s biggest equity fundraiser bids to garner more money than the three biggest-ever IPOs combinedBusiness live – latest updatesKenneth Rogoff: will AI create a permanent underclass?Google’s parent company, Alphabet, has said it plans to raise up to $80bn (£59bn) in equity to fund its vast artificial intelligence infrastructure investments, raising further questions over the economics of the AI boom.The move, the largest equity fundraising ever according to analysts, includes a $10bn share sale to the US investment group Berkshire Hathaway, which was led until last year by Warren Buffett. clusters.title: Mexican police tear-gas teachers' protest 10 days before World Cup

items.title: Mexican police tear-gas teachers' protest 10 days before World Cup
items.standfirst: Mexico City police hurled tear gas at protesting teachers to keep them from reaching the historic square where the "Fan fest" for the 2026 World Cup is under construction. The clash started when teachers broke through one of the metal barriers that have been set up at the entrance to the Zocalo plaza, a block from the government palace and a giant screen where fans will watch Mexico's first World Cup game on June 11.
items.content: Mexico City police hurled tear gas at protesting teachers to keep them from reaching the historic square where the "Fan fest" for the 2026 World Cup is under construction. The clash started when teachers broke through one of the metal barriers that have been set up at the entrance to the Zocalo plaza, a block from the government palace and a giant screen where fans will watch Mexico's first World Cup game on June 11.

items.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
items.standfirst: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June.“This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...
items.content: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June.“This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...

ai_summary: Mexico City police teargas teachers' protest

clusters.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty

items.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
items.standfirst: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of TuesdayDenmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition.Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Green Left and the centrist Moderates ended two months of uncertainty after March elections in which 12 parties won seats in parliament. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: U.K. bars left-wing influencers from entering country ahead of speaking gigs
items.standfirst: The progressive political commentators Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker said they had been barred from entering the United Kingdom to speak at events. Uygur, who co-hosts the YouTube series “The Young Turks,” and his nephew Piker, who broadcasts his commentary on the video platform Twitch, said on social media they had been blocked because of their views about Israel. NBC News' Sahil Kapur reports.

NBC News' Sahil Kapur reports.

clusters.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
clusters.neutral_headline: Colombia Presidential Election First Round Results

items.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
items.standfirst: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election.That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...
items.content: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election.That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...

items.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
items.standfirst: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksA far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting.With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014 and is backed by the current leftwing president, Gustavo Petro. Continue reading...
items.content: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksA far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting.With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014 and is backed by the current leftwing president, Gustavo Petro. Continue reading...

items.title: Colombia’s Petro sows doubt about election as favored successor heads to runoff against pro-Trump rival
items.standfirst: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella
items.content: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella

clusters.neutral_headline: French Navy Seizes Russian Oil Tanker

items.title: Britain helps French navy seize Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker
items.standfirst: UK forces helped the French navy intercept an oil tanker under international sanctions that was coming from Russia, alleged to be part of its shadow fleet.
items.content: UK forces helped the French navy intercept an oil tanker under international sanctions that was coming from Russia, alleged to be part of its shadow fleet.

items.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in high seas, Macron says
items.standfirst: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.
items.content: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.

ai_summary: French navy intercepts oil tanker under international sanctions
Tanker was coming from Russia and part of shadow fleet
UK forces helped French navy with the interception
Tanker was named the Tagor and sailing from Russia
Seizure was announced by French President Emmanuel Macron
